title: Complaint Log
author: Anonymous
variants:
Every chest hinge
on this train
is slightly sticky.

Not enough to be broken.
Enough to notice.
I checked twelve carriages.
It is consistent across them.

A stuck hinge can be oiled.
A working hinge can be ignored.
This one cannot be either.

Slime leaves a film
that does not wash off.
I have tried.

Hot water, sand, dry rags.
The film is still there.
My boots squeak now.
They did not used to squeak.

There are no new boots
on the train.
I have looked.
The slimes are still there.
I step in them every morning.

Lanterns on the floor.
Candles in doorways.
Always at ankle height.

Whoever placed them
did not walk back through.
I have tripped sixteen times
that I will admit to.

I memorise where they are.
The carriages repeat
in new arrangements.
The lanterns move with them.
The bruises do not.

In carriage 213,
the lanterns are not
where I left them.

The one by the door
has moved to the other side.
The two at the back
are a hand's width closer.
One is missing.
A different one is here.

I waited three nights.
Nothing moved.
On the fourth I slept.
They moved.

Fifteen sleeping cars
I have surveyed.
I will sleep in four.

Car 2341, bench three:
snores with a whistle.
Car 6792, bench eight:
snores with a cough.
Car 9847, bench one:
snores the same tune
every night.

I sleep in the other four.
Some nights a new snorer
finds my bench.
Some nights an old one
moves to mine.
The system is failing.

A thing in iron armour.
It dropped a bone.

Three crossbowmen.
Between them, one emerald.
A thing in chainmail.
It dropped a potato.

The loot does not justify
the swing of the sword.
I keep swinging.

Once, by accident,
I dug out a dirt cart
and found a chest inside.

I have dug out
forty-three dirt carts since.
I have used shovels.
I have used my hands.
I have dug them slowly.
I have dug them fast.
They have all been empty.

I have tried not looking.
I have tried looking away.
I have tried digging blind.
I have tried whistling.
I have tried silence.
I have tried thinking of soup.
I have tried thinking of nothing.
The train knows
when I am looking.
